rulebook_metadata.name: ACT_MEDIATION_PARAMETER ATTRIBUTE_VALUE Resolution Rulebook
rulebook_metadata.description: Declarative rule map for resolving ATTRIBUTE_VALUE column entries in the ACT_MEDIATION_PARAMETER table. Derived from Java method getCorrespondingAttributeValue in Util.java, with explicit distinction between atomic expression values and composite key=value ATTRIBUTE_VALUE strings.
rulebook_metadata.entry_point_method: getCorrespondingAttributeValue(String attributeValue, String projectDTOJsonValue, Map<String, Object> session_information)
rulebook_metadata.added_in_v1_1: Added attribute_value_shapes to distinguish atomic ATTRIBUTE_VALUE expressions from composite semicolon-delimited key=value ATTRIBUTE_VALUE strings.

mapping_logic.description: The ATTRIBUTE_VALUE column can be either a single atomic DSL expression or a composite semicolon-delimited set of key=value pairs. For composite values, each 'value' side uses the same prefix-based DSL to instruct the engine how to resolve the final runtime value from the source JSON or session.

prefix_dispatch_table.description: Static/literal value. The text after 'VAL_' is returned as-is.
Returns the text after '$SPACE_' with spaces preserved (no trim applied at post-processing).
Session variable lookup. Reads value from session_information map using key after '$S_'.
Numeric extraction. Recursively resolves the remainder, then strips all non-digit characters (keeps '-' and digits).
Integer conversion. Recursively resolves the remainder, then calls Integer.valueOf().
Alphabetic extraction. Recursively resolves, then strips all non-alpha characters.
VAT calculation. Resolves the path, then applies 5% VAT (value + value*0.05). Formatted to 2 decimal places.
Percentage deduction. Format: $DEDUCTPER_<percentage>_<path>. Resolves path, deducts given percentage.
Concatenation. Splits remainder by semicolons, recursively resolves each part, concatenates all results.
prefix_dispatch_table.resolution: StringBuilder joining recurse(each part split by ';')

prefix_dispatch_table.description: Long conversion. Resolves path, applies mapping if $M_ present, converts to Long (handles decimal strings).
Float conversion. Resolves path, converts to Float.
Double conversion. Resolves path, converts to Double.
Short literal. Directly converts the text after '$SHO_' to Short (no recursion).
BigDecimal conversion. Resolves path, converts to BigDecimal.
Data-to-bytes conversion. Format: $BYTE_<numberPath>^<uomPath>. Resolves both, converts to bytes based on UOM (TB/GB/MB/KB).
Data-to-kilobytes conversion. Format: $KBYTE_<numberPath>^<uomPath>. Same as $BYTE_ but result in KB.
Time-to-seconds conversion. Format: $SEC_<numberPath>^<uomPath>.
Time-to-hours conversion. Format: $HOUR_<numberPath>^<uomPath>.
Any-valid check. Pipes-separated paths are resolved in order. Returns 'true' if ANY resolves to a valid (non-null, non-empty) value, else 'false'. Optional #mapping suffix.
prefix_dispatch_table.resolution: for each path split by '|': if recurse(path) is valid => 'true'; else => 'false'. Optional doMapping.
prefix_dispatch_table.example_output: Y (if path1 or path2 has a value)

prefix_dispatch_table.description: Any-valid-value. Like $ANYVALID_ but returns the ACTUAL first valid value found (not 'true'/'false').
prefix_dispatch_table.resolution: for each path split by '|': if recurse(path) is valid => return that value. Optional doMapping.

prefix_dispatch_table.description: Any-valid binary. Like $ANYVALID_ but returns '1' if any valid, '0' otherwise (no mapping support).
prefix_dispatch_table.resolution: for each path split by '|': if recurse(path) is valid => '1'; else => '0'

prefix_dispatch_table.description: Any-true boolean OR. Resolves each pipe-separated path. If ANY returns Boolean true => '1', else '0'. Optional #mapping.
prefix_dispatch_table.resolution: for each path split by '|': if Boolean.valueOf(recurse(path)) => '1'; else => '0'. Optional doMapping.

prefix_dispatch_table.description: All-true boolean AND. Resolves each pipe-separated path. If ALL return Boolean true => '1', else '0'. Optional #mapping.
prefix_dispatch_table.resolution: for each path split by '|': if ALL Boolean.valueOf(recurse(path)) => '1'; else => '0'. Optional doMapping.

prefix_dispatch_table.description: Map builder. Creates a key-value Map. Semicolons separate entries, equals separates key from value. Both sides are recursively resolved.
prefix_dispatch_table.resolution: for each pair split by ';': key=recurse(left of '='), val=recurse(right of '='). Return Map.

prefix_dispatch_table.description: Date parsing. Resolves path to a date string, parses using formats: ['MMM d, yyyy HH:mm:ss a', 'dd-MM-yyyy', 'dd/MM/yyyy', 'dd-MMM-yyyy']. Returns Date object.
Date formatting. Format: $DATEFORMAT_<outputFormat>^<path>. Resolves path (must resolve to Date), formats with given pattern.
prefix_dispatch_table.note: substring(13) is used — the underscore after 'DATEFORMAT' is included in the 12-char prefix

prefix_dispatch_table.description: Date re-formatting. Format: $FORMATDATE_<inputFormat>$<path>$<outputFormat>. Parses date string from path using inputFormat, then formats to outputFormat.
Current date/time. Text after '$NOW_' is the output format pattern. Returns formatted current timestamp.
Boolean conversion. Resolves path, converts result to Boolean.
Camel/Title case conversion. Resolves path, converts each word to Title Case (first char uppercase, rest lowercase).
prefix_dispatch_table.note: Skips conversion if result starts with '$'

prefix_dispatch_table.description: Direct JsonPath read. Reads directly from projectDTOJsonValue using the full path after prefix. If result is single-element JSONArray, returns that element as string. Otherwise returns full JSON.
JSON array read. Reads directly from projectDTOJsonValue using JsonPath. Returns the raw object (typically a list).

complex_method_dispatch.description: When no known prefix matches, the code checks for 'complex methods' using regex pattern '^\$[^_]+_'. These are methods applied AFTER the remaining path is resolved.

methods.description: String replacement. Params: ('oldText', 'newText'). Resolves the path after method prefix, then replaces occurrences.
Split to list. Params: ('delimiter'). Resolves the path, splits result by delimiter, returns as List.
Arithmetic operation. Params: ('operator', 'operand'). Resolves path, performs math. If operand starts with '$', it is also resolved.
methods.note: If second param starts with '$', it is recursively resolved (without the $ prefix)

methods.description: List index access. Params: ('index', optional 'mapping'). Resolves path (must return List), accesses element at index. Optional second param applies mapping.
Boolean XOR. Params: ('secondBooleanPath', optional 'mapping'). Resolves path, XORs with second boolean. If second starts with '$', resolved.

fallback_json_path_resolution.description: If no prefix matches and no complex method detected, the value is treated as a direct JsonPath expression against projectDTOJsonValue.
steps: 1. Check for '#' separator → split into path and mapping expression
2. Check for '+' separator → AND boolean logic: resolve each path, Boolean.valueOf each, AND all together
4. Convert JSON arrays to comma-separated string via arrayToComaString()
5. Apply mapping via getinternalMappedValue() if '#' was found
6. If result starts with '$EVAL_' → recursively resolve the remainder
fallback_json_path_resolution.example_output: the value at that JSON path in the source DTO

$M_suffix.description: Pre-processing modifier. If attributeValue contains '$M_', it splits: left side becomes the new attributeValue, right side becomes the mapping to apply AFTER resolution.

post_processing.trim: All String results are trimmed UNLESS prefix was '$SPACE'
post_processing.coma_replacement: All occurrences of literal '$COMA$' in String results are replaced with ','
post_processing.purpose: Use $COMA$ in values where a literal comma is needed but would otherwise be interpreted as a delimiter

attribute_value_shapes.description: Top-level shapes that an ACT_MEDIATION_PARAMETER.ATTRIBUTE_VALUE can take. This section prevents the LLM from incorrectly wrapping every value as key=value or incorrectly treating DTO/source fields as VAL_ literals.
why_this_matters: ATTRIBUTE_NAME already provides the database-level key for normal/atomic rows, so ATTRIBUTE_VALUE should be only the compiled expression such as VAL_123 or allowances.sms.freebies.
Container attributes such as poAttributes store multiple sub-keys inside the ATTRIBUTE_VALUE string, so they require key=<expression>; fragments.
The same compiled expression grammar is used in both shapes; only the outer storage shape changes.

atomic_expression.description: The entire ATTRIBUTE_VALUE column is one DSL expression. Use this when ATTRIBUTE_NAME itself is the target attribute.
when_to_use: Adding a normal new attribute row, for example: add attribute gundu with value 123.
Updating an existing normal attribute value where the full ATTRIBUTE_VALUE is one expression.
The user does not say inside/within/under an existing composite/container attribute.

examples.user_intent: Add new attribute gundu with value 123
examples.meaning: The attribute gundu has static literal value 123.

examples.user_intent: Add new attribute gundu with DTO field allowances.sms.freebies
examples.meaning: The attribute gundu reads from the DTO/source field allowances.sms.freebies.

examples.user_intent: Add new attribute AddToBillFlagCopy. If addToBill is false set false otherwise true.
examples.meaning: The attribute reads source field addToBill and maps false to false, otherwise true.

composite_key_value_expression.description: The ATTRIBUTE_VALUE column contains one or more semicolon-delimited key=value segments. Use this when ATTRIBUTE_NAME is a container/composite attribute such as poAttributes or productSpecAttribute_X.
when_to_use: The user says inside existing poAttributes, inside poAttributes, within poAttributes, under poAttributes, or similar container wording.
Oracle inspection shows the target ATTRIBUTE_NAME already has a packed key=value;key=value; ATTRIBUTE_VALUE.
The task is append_subkey or full compound ATTRIBUTE_VALUE generation.

append_subkey_rule.description: For append_subkey, the DSL compiler should return only the RHS compiled_expression. The SQL layer later wraps it as append_key=<compiledExpression>; and appends it to the preserved existing ATTRIBUTE_VALUE.
append_subkey_rule.important: Even if the sub-key value is a fixed literal, operation_kind remains append_subkey, not fixed_literal, because the storage target is a composite container.

examples.user_intent: Add CustomerType with value 123 inside existing poAttributes
examples.meaning: Append sub-key CustomerType whose value is the literal 123.
wrong_outputs: VAL_123 as the whole poAttributes value

examples.user_intent: Add CustomerType with DTO field customer.type inside existing poAttributes
examples.meaning: Append sub-key CustomerType whose value is read from DTO/source field customer.type.

examples.user_intent: Add CustomerType with value if subscriberType is PREPAID show 1 else 0 inside existing poAttributes
examples.meaning: Append sub-key CustomerType whose value is mapped from source field subscriberType.

examples.user_intent: Create full compound productSpecAttribute_1 with static attribute name, byte-converted value, and mapped action
examples.meaning: A full composite ATTRIBUTE_VALUE with three key=value segments.

decision_rules_for_llm: First decide whether the target ATTRIBUTE_VALUE shape is atomic_expression or composite_key_value_expression.
If the user says inside/within/under existing poAttributes, choose composite_key_value_expression and operation_kind append_subkey.
If the target is a normal ATTRIBUTE_NAME and not a container, choose atomic_expression and store only the compiled expression.
After deciding the shape, compile the value expression using prefix_dispatch_table and fallback_json_path_resolution.
VAL_ is only for static/literal values. Never use VAL_ for DTO/source field paths.
Plain paths with no prefix are DTO/source field reads by fallback JSON path resolution.
Mappings use sourceField#conditionValue|resultValue,ELSE|elseValue. The text before # is the source field, not the target attribute name or append key.

string_manipulation_rules.description: Rules governing composite key=value ATTRIBUTE_VALUE strings and delimiter behavior. Atomic expression ATTRIBUTE_VALUE rows do not use the outer key=value; wrapper.

attribute_value_structure.note: Each key=value pair within ATTRIBUTE_VALUE uses semicolons as pair separators and equals as key-value separators.
attribute_value_structure.not_for_atomic_values: Atomic rows store only the compiled expression, for example VAL_123 or allowances.sms.freebies.

delimiter_hierarchy.purpose: Primary pair separator in ATTRIBUTE_VALUE. Also used as part separator in $CONCAT_ expressions.
Separates attribute key from its resolution expression within a pair.
Mapping/conditional separator. Left side is the path/expression, right side is the mapping rules.
delimiter_hierarchy.context: After a JSON path expression to apply value transformation

delimiter_hierarchy.purpose: Multiple uses: (1) Separates alternatives in $ANYVALID_/$ANYTRUE_ etc. (2) Separates condition from result in mapping expressions (old|new).
delimiter_hierarchy.context: Inside mapping expressions and multi-path checks

delimiter_hierarchy.purpose: Separates multiple mapping entries in a mapping expression.
Separates compound parameters (e.g., number^uom in $BYTE_, format^path in $DATEFORMAT_).
Prefix indicator for DSL tokens AND separator in $FORMATDATE_ expressions.
delimiter_hierarchy.context: Always starts a dynamic token ($S_, $NUM_, etc.)

rules: Conditions are matched case-insensitively against the resolved value
'null' (literal string) matches when resolved value is Java null
'ELSE' is a wildcard catch-all that matches anything
If result starts with '$EVAL_', the remainder is recursively resolved as a new expression
If result is '$SKIP', null is returned (used to intentionally omit the attribute)
If no condition matches, the original resolved value is returned unchanged
Chained mappings use '#' separator: 'firstMapping#secondMapping' — second mapping is appended as tail to result of first

special_tokens_in_values.$EVAL_: When a mapping result starts with this, strip it and recursively resolve the remainder through getCorrespondingAttributeValue
special_tokens_in_values.$SKIP: Return null — signals that this attribute should be excluded from the final output
special_tokens_in_values.$COMA$: Literal comma placeholder. Replaced with ',' in post-processing. Use when a comma is part of data, not a delimiter.
special_tokens_in_values.$SPACE_: Preserves spaces and skips trim in post-processing

null_or_empty_input.behavior: If attributeValue is null or empty string, method returns null immediately
null_or_empty_input.check: isValidObject() returns false for null or empty string ''

json_path_not_found.behavior: readJsonPath() catches all exceptions and returns null. The attribute will have null value.
json_path_not_found.logging: Logs 'path X was not available in provided JSON Ignoring the value' at DEBUG level

mapping_no_match.behavior: If no mapping condition matches (and no ELSE), getMappedValue returns the ORIGINAL resolved value unchanged
mapping_no_match.logging: Logs error 'wrong Mapping for value [...] and mapping options [...]'

null_session_variable.behavior: If $S_ key not found in session map, returns null
null_session_variable.note: session_information.get() returns null, which is preserved

type_conversion_failure.$INT_: Throws NumberFormatException if resolved value is not numeric
type_conversion_failure.$FLOAT_: Returns null if input is null
type_conversion_failure.$DEC_: Returns null if input is null

array_handling.behavior: If JsonPath returns a JSON array like '[val1, val2]', arrayToComaString converts it to 'val1,val2'
array_handling.single_element_jsonpath: For $JSONPATH_, if JSONArray has exactly 1 element, returns just that element as string

boolean_evaluation.$ANYTRUE_/$ALLTRUE_: Uses Boolean.valueOf() which returns false for any string that is not exactly 'true' (case-insensitive)
boolean_evaluation.plus_operator: When attributeValue contains '+', each segment is resolved and AND-ed together as booleans

$BYTE_and_$SEC_null_guard.behavior: $SEC_ and $HOUR_ check isValidObject(number) before conversion. If number is null/empty, value remains null.
$BYTE_and_$SEC_null_guard.$BYTE_/$KBYTE_: No null guard — will throw NPE if number or uom resolves to null

recursive_depth.note: No explicit recursion limit. Circular references (e.g., $EVAL_ pointing to itself) will cause StackOverflowError.

$CAMEL_skip_condition.behavior: If resolved value starts with '$', convertToCamelCase is skipped (value returned as-is). This prevents corrupting unresolved tokens.

resolution_trace: 1. Full ATTRIBUTE_VALUE is split by ';' into pairs
→ No prefix: treated as JSON path → readJsonPath() → returns 'true'/'false'/value

resolution_trace: 1. Prefix '$ANYVALID_' detected → remainder has '#' → split: paths='$.services.mobile|$.services.fixed', mapping='true|Y,false|N'
3. Loop: recurse '$.services.mobile' → found valid value → result = 'true'

few_shot_examples.processing_description: Read base price from JSON, add 5 to it.
Read promoCard boolean, map false to a recursively evaluated expression, true to another.
resolution_trace: 6. Since last match is at index < total entries, tail is NOT appended
7. Back in doMapping: value starts with '$EVAL_' → strip → 'VAL_DISCOUNT_FRAMEWORK'

Get current date/time formatted as yyyy-MM-dd.
Read customer name, replace spaces with underscores.

few_shot_examples.scenario: Full multi-pair ATTRIBUTE_VALUE for a mediation template
few_shot_examples.processing_description: Compound entry: static attribute name + dynamic value resolved from JSON with conditional mapping.
resolution_trace: Pair 3: key='attributeAction', expr='status#ACTIVE|Add,INACTIVE|Remove' → path 'status' with mapping ACTIVE→Add, INACTIVE→Remove

when_building_attribute_value: 1. Identify what runtime values are needed (from JSON path, session, or static)
2. Choose the appropriate prefix for each value based on required type/transformation
3. Structure as semicolon-separated key=value pairs if compound
4. Use '#' after a JSON path to add conditional mapping (condition|result,...)
5. Use 'ELSE|$SKIP' when the attribute should be excluded if no condition matches
6. Use 'VAL_' for any static/literal text that should not be resolved from JSON
7. Use '$CONCAT_' with ';' separators when combining multiple resolved values
8. Use '$M_' suffix BEFORE processing when you need to map the ENTIRE expression's result
9. Always end compound ATTRIBUTE_VALUE strings with a trailing semicolon
